data.title: Migrate skill evals to 3-tier testing pyramid
data.description:
### Goal
Replace flaky LLM-graded skill evals with a reliable 3-tier testing architecture.

### Problem
Current skill evals use llm-rubric (LLM-as-judge) assertions that are non-deterministic — the same test passes or fails depending on random LLM output. This blocks commits unpredictably and wastes developer time. Root cause: two layers of non-determinism (generator LLM + grader LLM).

### Proposed Architecture

**Tier 1: Deterministic structural tests (commit-time gate)**
- Grep-based tests that verify SKILL.md contains required signal formats, routing rules, gate conditions
- Already have many of these (test_sprint_gap_classification.py, test-sub-agent-guard.sh, etc.)
- Convert remaining llm-rubric assertions that check for specific signals into structural tests

**Tier 2: Deterministic promptfoo assertions (commit-time, optional)**
- Replace llm-rubric with contains/javascript/regex assertions in promptfoo configs
- Eliminates grader LLM non-determinism entirely
- Estimate ~80% of current llm-rubric assertions can be converted

**Tier 3: LLM-graded tests (daily CI only, with --repeat)**
- Keep llm-rubric only for genuinely subjective behavioral tests
- Run with --repeat 3 and threshold 0.6 (pass if 2/3 runs pass)
- Never block commits — create tickets on regression

### Additional Improvements
- Add temperature: 0 to all eval provider configs
- Build a skill-contract-validator (deterministic) for cross-file reference integrity
- Evaluate cc-plugin-eval pattern for trigger precision testing

### Success Criteria
- Zero flaky eval failures at commit time
- All signal-format assertions converted to deterministic tests
- Daily CI catches genuine eval regressions within 24h
- Eval pass rate >95% on --repeat 3 for Tier 3 tests
